The Ur-Dragon.plan: Dragon tribal ramp with treasure support; curve to high-impact dragons and anthem effects.
The Ur-Dragon.notes: Prioritise dragon cost reducers, tutors like Sarkhan's Triumph, and board-wide haste enablers.

flagship.loveReason: Explosive dragon turns, eminence value from the command zone, and a timeless five-color ‘big flying things’ fantasy that still feels competitive.
flagship.bestFor: Players who want splashy combat kills, treasure-based fixing, and a recognizable 5-color dragon identity.
winPaths: Overrun the air with dragons after ramping under eminence
Treasure bursts into a one-shot alpha with haste or mass pump
Incremental value until a critical mass of fliers closes the game
traps: Too many expensive dragons and no bridge turns
Skipping interaction because curve is ‘already tight’
Weak mana fixing in real 5-color games
upgradePriority: 1. Mana base + fixing (lands, treasure, cheap ramp)
2. Cost reduction and tutors that find dragons on time
3. Protection and interaction that preserves your engine
4. Finishers and anthem effects that convert a wide board
openingPlan: Turn 1: land + cheap rock or setup where possible
Turn 2: ramp or early interaction; deploy a clock if safe
Turn 3+: begin deploying dragons or hold up protection based on table speed
flagship.tableReputation: Often respected as a ‘big mana dragon’ deck — can draw early removal if you goldfish too openly.
flagship.communityHeadline: How people build The Ur-Dragon now
flagship.communitySubhead: Explore live ManaTap lists — compare curves, treasures, and dragon density.

Edgar Markov.plan: Low-curve vampire aggro with token multipliers and +1/+1 counters.
Edgar Markov.notes: Maximise vampire ETB token makers and haste support.

flagship.loveReason: Free eminence tokens and an aggressive Mardu clock that looks casual but punishes slow keeps.
flagship.bestFor: Players who enjoy wide boards, combat pressure from turn one, and tribal sequencing.
winPaths: Snowball tokens into anthems and drain
Chip damage into a fast lethal swing after a stalled board
Exploit eminence to never ‘do nothing’ early
traps: Too many 5+ mana vampires in a low-land opener
Skipping protection for your commander in removal-heavy pods
Diluting tribal density with off-theme ‘goodstuff’
upgradePriority: 4. Top-end only after consistency is solved
openingPlan: Turn 1: land + one-drop or strong rock
Turn 2: curve vampires; deploy pressure before answers stabilize
Turn 3: start converting tokens into real damage or hold interaction
flagship.tableReputation: Reads as aggressive — sometimes underestimated until the board is suddenly lethal.
flagship.communitySubhead: See popular aggro vs mid lists and card choices on ManaTap.

Atraxa, Praetors' Voice.plan: Choose a focused proliferate shell (infect, superfriends, or +1/+1 counters) and double down.
Atraxa, Praetors' Voice.notes: Support with protective spells and repeatable proliferate engines.

flagship.loveReason: Flexible four-color value with proliferate — one of Commander’s most iconic ‘pick a lane and snowball’ legends.
flagship.bestFor: Players who want engines, incremental advantage, and a deck that rewards tight synergy choices.
winPaths: Poison / infect closers with repeatable proliferate
Superfriends ultimates and walkers that stabilize
+1/+1 counters and combat kills after engine online
traps: Split identity — infect cards plus random walkers with no plan
Too Cute™ value pieces without a finisher
Greedy mana that can’t cast Atraxa on curve in fast metas
upgradePriority: 1. Pick ONE primary plan and align lands/interaction
2. Ramp / fixing that supports four colors reliably
3. Protection and recursion for key engines
4. Wincons that close once you are ahead
openingPlan: Turn 1: land + setup (rock or cheap enabler)
Turn 2: deploy an engine piece or interaction
Turn 3+: land Atraxa when safe; proliferate starts next cycle
flagship.tableReputation: Threat magnet — many tables will try to answer engines early.
flagship.communitySubhead: Infect, superfriends, and counters builds — compare real decklists.

Krenko, Mob Boss.plan: Explosive goblin token swarm leveraging extra damage on ETB/attack.
Krenko, Mob Boss.notes: Include Impact Tremors effects and haste enablers to snowball.

flagship.loveReason: Single-card exponential tokens — Krenko turns goblins into a combo-ish swarm that wins out of nowhere.
flagship.bestFor: Players who love snowball math, combat wins, and mono-red pressure.
winPaths: Tap Krenko for a massive board, then haste alpha
Ping/ladder damage with ETB/attack payoffs
Sacrifice lines that convert tokens into lethal drains or damage
traps: All-in with zero protection into a wrath
Too few lands / rocks for a commander-centric plan
Filler goblins that don’t advance the critical turn
upgradePriority: 1. Lands + fast mana to deploy Krenko early
4. Light interaction that protects the combo turn
openingPlan: Turn 1: land + one-mana goblin or mana source
Turn 2: curve or ramp toward Krenko
Turn 3+: deploy Krenko and start doubling — hold protection if you expect interaction
flagship.tableReputation: Highly targeted — looks scarier the longer it stays up.
flagship.communitySubhead: Compare swarm vs combo-adjacent goblin lists from the community.

Kaalia of the Vast.plan: Cheat massive angels, demons, and dragons into play while protecting Kaalia.
Kaalia of the Vast.notes: Emphasise protective equipment/auras and game-ending bombs.

Pantlaza, Sun-Favored.plan: Dinosaur tribal ramp with discover and blink synergies.
Pantlaza, Sun-Favored.notes: Lean on cost reducers and ramp to chain discover triggers.

Sauron, the Dark Lord.plan: Amass-oriented zombie tokens with wheels and reanimation loops.
Sauron, the Dark Lord.notes: Support with discard draw sevens and large payoff enchantments.

Yuriko, the Tiger's Shadow.plan: Tempo ninjutsu deck leveraging evasive one-drops and top-deck manipulation.
Yuriko, the Tiger's Shadow.notes: Include bounce/flicker to reuse triggers and keep curve extremely low.

flagship.loveReason: Command-zone card advantage that rewards tight curves and sneak-attack sequencing — uniquely Dimir tempo.
flagship.bestFor: Spikes and spiky-casual players who enjoy micro-decisions, tempo plays, and topdeck manipulation.
winPaths: Drain and chip from Yuriko triggers into a decisive turn
Leverage unblockable ninjas + extra turns/bounce lines (where legal/fit)
Value ninjutsu loops with cheap evasive creatures
traps: Too many expensive spells ruining curve
No bounce/flicker to reuse enter-combat triggers
Greedy keeps without interaction vs faster decks
upgradePriority: 4. Finishers that don’t wreck the curve
openingPlan: Turn 1: evasive creature or setup
Turn 2: hold mana for interaction or advanceboard
Turn 3+: ninjutsu online — prioritize mana efficiency each attack step
flagship.tableReputation: Often respected as fast — can draw removal if you telegraph huge turns.
flagship.communitySubhead: See curve choices and interaction suites from real ManaTap decks.

Lathril, Blade of the Elves.plan: Elf tribal tokens into overrun finishers with mana engine support.
Lathril, Blade of the Elves.notes: Ensure sufficient anthem effects and elf-based card draw.

Kenrith, the Returned King.plan: Flexible political toolbox leveraging activated abilities and combo lines.
Kenrith, the Returned King.notes: Support with mana sinks and tutors that fetch specific ability combos.

Giada, Font of Hope.plan: Mono-white angel ramp with +1/+1 counters and lifegain support.
Giada, Font of Hope.notes: Add artifact ramp to cast expensive angels on curve.

Jodah, the Unifier.plan: Five-color legendary tribal with cascade-style value.
Jodah, the Unifier.notes: Prioritise mana fixing and legendary payoff permanents.

Miirym, Sentinel Wyrm.plan: Temur dragon tribal doubling key ETB effects via clones.
Miirym, Sentinel Wyrm.notes: Include token-friendly payoff like Parallel Lives and treasure ramp.

The Wise Mothman.plan: Mutant-themed mill with proliferate and +1/+1 counter synergies.
The Wise Mothman.notes: Use self-mill for value and rad counter payoffs.

Nekusar, the Mindrazer.plan: Wheel-based group slug that punishes opposing card draw.
Nekusar, the Mindrazer.notes: Ensure redundancy in wheel effects and damage doublers.

Y'shtola, Night's Blessed.plan: Spellslinger control leveraging noncreature spells for damage and lifegain.
Y'shtola, Night's Blessed.notes: Keep curve low with instant-speed interaction and storm-style payoffs.

Isshin, Two Heavens as One.plan: Attack-trigger doubling with token makers, equipment, and extra combats.
avoid: board wipes that reset your own army
Isshin, Two Heavens as One.notes: Prioritise creatures with impactful attack triggers and mass haste.

flagship.loveReason: Doubles attack triggers — Mardu combat turns go from ‘good’ to ‘unfair’ when sequencing is tight.
flagship.bestFor: Players who like combat steps, token go-wide, and extra-combat blowouts.
winPaths: Extra combat steps + token producers
Sublime equipment/sword packages that scale triggers
Go-wide aggression that punishes tapped-out opponents
traps: Too few bodies to reliably attack profitable
Wraths with no recovery plan
Trigger density without a way to force meaningful combats
upgradePriority: 1. Low-CMC creatures with great attack triggers
openingPlan: Turn 1: creature or rock
Turn 2: deploy threat or develop tokens
Turn 3+: land Isshin when safe and start doubling triggers
flagship.tableReputation: Combat-focused — looks mid-threat until extra combats appear.
flagship.communitySubhead: Compare token-heavy vs equipment packages in public lists.

Hakbal of the Surging Soul.plan: Merfolk tribal with +1/+1 counters, explore, and evasion.
Hakbal of the Surging Soul.notes: Include islandwalk enablers and tempo plays.

Ulalek, Fused Atrocity.plan: Colorless Eldrazi ramp with sacrifice loops and ping payoffs.
Ulalek, Fused Atrocity.notes: Stack mana rocks and token producers to fuel annihilator threats.

Ms. Bumbleflower.plan: Wheel-centric group hug with +1/+1 counters and token production.
Ms. Bumbleflower.notes: Support with pillow-fort pieces and payoff creatures that scale with draw.

Teysa Karlov.plan: Orzhov aristocrats strategy doubling death triggers to drain opponents and generate value from creature sacrifice.
Teysa Karlov.notes: Prioritize Blood Artist effects, token generators like Bitterblossom, and free sacrifice outlets such as Viscera Seer. Key engines include Pitiless Plunderer and Grave Pact. Keep curve low for repeatable sacrifice loops.

Korvold, Fae-Cursed King.plan: Jund sacrifice engine that turns permanents into cards and +1/+1 counters, often combo-finishing through treasure or food loops.
Korvold, Fae-Cursed King.notes: Dockside Extortionist and Mayhem Devil are premium. Include fetchlands and token producers to ensure consistent sacrifice fodder. Avoid too many nonpermanent spells; Korvold thrives on permanents.

flagship.loveReason: A Commander that rewards smart sacrifice sequencing with massive card flow — the Jund value/combo sweet spot.
flagship.bestFor: Players who want toolsy gameplay and high synergy with ramp, treasures, and aristocrats.
winPaths: Treasure/food loops with payoffs that close
Mayhem-style chip damage while drawing ahead
Combat kills after Korvold grows out of range
traps: Too many instants/sorceries with low permanent density
No fodder — sac outlets without bodies
Overextending into wraths with no rebuild
upgradePriority: 1. Lands + cheap artifacts/creatures you’re happy to bin
2. Token producers and free sac outlets
3. Payoffs that reward permanents leaving play
openingPlan: Turn 1: land + rock or dork
Turn 2: develop fodder or interaction
Turn 3+: Korvold turns fodder into cards — sequence attacks vs protection
flagship.tableReputation: Often treated as a top-tier value deck — expect answers.
flagship.communitySubhead: See sacrifice density, treasure packages, and finisher choices in decklists.

Meren of Clan Nel Toth.plan: Graveyard recursion engine using experience counters to repeatedly reanimate value creatures.
Meren of Clan Nel Toth.notes: Include Sakura-Tribe Elder, Fleshbag Marauder, and Spore Frog loops. Prioritize cheap creatures that sacrifice themselves. Avoid too many high-CMC threats that don't generate immediate value.

flagship.loveReason: Repeatable grindy value from the graveyard — experience makes your recursion scale as the game goes long.
flagship.bestFor: Grindy value players who like aristocrats, sac outlets, and inevitability.
winPaths: Soft locks with ETB denial and grindy drain
Incremental value creatures looped each end step
Sacrifice combos once experience is high enough
traps: Too few creatures to fuel experience reliably
Too top-heavy — clunky creatures that don’t ETB
Weak to graveyard hate with no backup plan
upgradePriority: 1. Cheap creatures that die for value
openingPlan: Turn 1: dork or setup
Turn 2: early value creature or ramp
Turn 3+: Meren + start trading for experience — play for grind
flagship.tableReputation: Looks slow — becomes scary once experience clocks in.
flagship.communitySubhead: See aristocrats density and end-step lines in real lists.

Animar, Soul of Elements.plan: Creature combo deck reducing creature costs with +1/+1 counters to enable explosive chains and combo finishes.
Animar, Soul of Elements.notes: Ancestral Statue enables infinite counters. Prioritize low-cost creatures and ETB value like Cloud of Faeries. Animar rewards creature density above all else.

Prosper, Tome-Bound.plan: Rakdos value engine generating Treasures from exile-based card advantage.
Prosper, Tome-Bound.notes: Include Jeska's Will, Reckless Impulse effects, and Marionette Master as a finisher. Prosper thrives when nearly every turn generates exile value.

flagship.loveReason: Treasures from impulse draw — one of the best Rakdos ‘every turn is a mini-game’ commanders for spellslinger value.
flagship.bestFor: Players who enjoy sequencing exile turns, treasures, and flexible win lines.
winPaths: Treasure payoffs and big mana turns
Artifact/aristocrat drains once artifacts pile up
Burn/spell finishes after card advantage accrues
traps: Playing like mono-aggro without exile payoffs
Too few treasures or ways to use them
Ignoring interaction and dying on key turns
upgradePriority: 3. Flexible removal that keeps you alive
openingPlan: Turn 1: land + rock or setup
Turn 2: impulse/exile or early interaction
Turn 3+: deploy Prosper and start converting exile into tempo
flagship.tableReputation: Reads as value — can look scary once treasure count ticks up.
flagship.communitySubhead: Spell vs treasure-focused shells — browse ManaTap lists.

Winota, Joiner of Forces.plan: Explosive combat strategy cheating powerful Humans into play via non-Human attackers.
Winota, Joiner of Forces.notes: Include cheap non-Humans like Ornithopter and powerful Humans like Angrath's Marauders. Avoid diluting Human count. Fast mana accelerates lethal turns.

Selvala, Heart of the Wilds.plan: Green ramp engine generating explosive mana through high-power creatures, often enabling combo wins.
Selvala, Heart of the Wilds.notes: Umbral Mantle enables infinite mana. Include high-power creatures like Regal Force. Selvala rewards power-based scaling.

Urza, Lord High Artificer.plan: Artifact combo/control deck leveraging artifact density for mana and value.
Urza, Lord High Artificer.notes: Include cheap artifacts and mana rocks. Dramatic Reversal combos with Isochron Scepter. Urza requires high artifact density to function.

Najeela, the Blade-Blossom.plan: Warrior tribal combat combo deck capable of infinite combat steps with sufficient mana.
Najeela, the Blade-Blossom.notes: Derevi, Empyrial Tactician or Druids' Repository enable infinite combats. Prioritize cheap Warriors and combat enablers.

Arcades, the Strategist.plan: Defender tribal deck turning high-toughness creatures into card draw and combat threats.
Arcades, the Strategist.notes: Wall of Omens and Overgrown Battlement are core. Focus on cheap defenders to maximize draw triggers.

Wilhelt, the Rotcleaver.plan: Zombie tribal aristocrats deck generating decayed tokens and leveraging death triggers.
Wilhelt, the Rotcleaver.notes: Include Rooftop Storm and Gravecrawler loops. Wilhelt rewards Zombie density and sacrifice engines.

Sythis, Harvest's Hand.plan: Enchantress value engine drawing cards and gaining life from enchantment casts.
Sythis, Harvest's Hand.notes: Argothian Enchantress and Sterling Grove are premium. Sythis thrives with 30+ enchantments.

Kess, Dissident Mage.plan: Grixis spell-slinger combo deck recasting key instants and sorceries from graveyard.
Kess, Dissident Mage.notes: Demonic Consultation and Underworld Breach combos are common. Protect Kess to maximize spell recursion.

Brago, King Eternal.plan: Blink control deck abusing ETB triggers through repeated flicker effects.
Brago, King Eternal.notes: Strionic Resonator and mana rocks enable infinite flicker loops. Prioritize strong ETB creatures.

Chatterfang, Squirrel General.plan: Token swarm aristocrats deck converting token production into removal and combo finishes.
Chatterfang, Squirrel General.notes: Pitiless Plunderer and Earthcraft are powerful. Chatterfang scales explosively with token multipliers.

Zada, Hedron Grinder.plan: Mono-red spell combo deck copying single-target spells across a wide board for explosive turns.
Zada, Hedron Grinder.notes: Expedite and Crimson Wisps become massive draw engines. Maintain wide board presence.

Feather, the Redeemed.plan: Boros value engine recurring targeted instants to generate continuous card advantage.
Feather, the Redeemed.notes: Defiant Strike and Shelter are core. Feather requires cheap spells that target your own creatures.

Omnath, Locus of Creation.plan: Landfall value deck generating incremental advantage and burst mana from land drops.
Omnath, Locus of Creation.notes: Fetchlands maximize triggers. Include Scute Swarm and Omnath synergy loops.

flagship.loveReason: Landfall lines that stack advantage every turn — a flagship four-color ‘lands matter’ fantasy with explosive turns.
flagship.bestFor: Players who love ramp, sequencing lands, and engine-heavy midrange.
winPaths: Elemental/token swarm and landfall payoffs
Combo-style lines that leverage extra land drops
Incremental life + board until opponents run out of answers
traps: Greedy manabase without enough fixing
Too few lands for a land-centric plan
Overcommitting before wraths reset the board
upgradePriority: 1. Fetchlands + duals/shocks that support four colors
openingPlan: Turn 1: land + dork/rock when possible
Turn 2: ramp or interaction depending on pod speed
Turn 3+: deploy Omnath and chain land drops for value triggers
flagship.tableReputation: Often respected as a high-ceiling deck — can be targeted once engines appear.
flagship.communitySubhead: Compare landfall suites and finishers from ManaTap decks.

Tasigur, the Golden Fang.plan: Sultai control/combo deck leveraging delve and political recursion.
Tasigur, the Golden Fang.notes: Fill graveyard efficiently. Infinite mana with Deadeye Navigator loops is common.

Muldrotha, the Gravetide.plan: Permanent-based recursion engine replaying permanents from graveyard each turn.
Muldrotha, the Gravetide.notes: Include permanent-based removal and ramp like Seal of Primordium and Sakura-Tribe Elder. Avoid too many instants and sorceries.
